text: A 77-year-old man with metastatic prostate cancer reports worsening back pain. He asks for meds exactly on time and is irritable. Most likely explanation?
rationale: Dx: Undertreated pain. Patients with chronic cancer pain often display behaviors misinterpreted as drug-seeking when pain is not controlled[span_16](end_span)[span_17](end_span).

text: A 21-year-old man with status epilepticus (seizure >5 mins) is unresponsive with mouth/arm twitching. First-line pharmacotherapy?
rationale: Dx: Status Epilepticus. [span_18](start_span)[span_19](start_span)Tx: Benzodiazepines (e.g., lorazepam) are first-line therapy[span_18](end_span)[span_19](end_span).

text: A 4-year-old boy has fever, sore throat, neck stiffness, and trismus (unable to open mouth). Likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Retropharyngeal abscess (RPA). [span_20](start_span)[span_21](start_span)Classic in kids 2-4 after URI; presents with trismus and neck pain on extension[span_20](end_span)[span_21](end_span).

text: A 56-year-old with palpable purpura, arthralgia, and Raynaud's. Labs: Positive cryoglobulins, low C4, RF positive. Most appropriate next test?
rationale: Dx: Cryoglobulinemic vasculitis. [span_22](start_span)[span_23](start_span)90% of cases are associated with Hepatitis C infection[span_22](end_span)[span_23](end_span).

text: A 30-year-old woman with Bipolar I taking Lithium is now pregnant. What fetal anomaly is most specifically associated with Lithium?
rationale: Risk: Lithium in the first trimester increases the risk of cardiac abnormalities, specifically Ebstein anomaly[span_24](end_span)[span_25](end_span).

text: A 25-year-old female fears embarrassment or scrutiny at work presentations and office parties. Most likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Social anxiety disorder (Social phobia). [span_26](start_span)[span_27](start_span)Persistent fear of social/performance situations resulting in humiliation[span_26](end_span)[span_27](end_span).

text: Regarding the 25-year-old with social anxiety disorder, what is the recommended first-line treatment?
rationale: Tx: First-line for social anxiety is CBT. [span_28](start_span)[span_29](start_span)Second-line include SSRIs or beta-blockers[span_28](end_span)[span_29](end_span).

text: A 64-year-old man on prochlorperazine feels restless and cannot stop moving his legs. Which med could have prevented this?
rationale: Dx: Akathisia. [span_30](start_span)[span_31](start_span)Prevention is most reliably maintained using benzodiazepines like lorazepam[span_30](end_span)[span_31](end_span).

text: 72yo asymptomatic woman. Duplex: complete occlusion of right ICA, mild stenosis of left ICA. Best management?
rationale: Tx: Medical therapy only (aspirin, statin, BP control). [span_32](start_span)[span_33](start_span)CEA is not beneficial for complete occlusion in asymptomatic patients[span_32](end_span)[span_33](end_span).

text: Which medication is associated with the highest risk of developing diabetes mellitus in an obese hypertensive patient?
rationale: Risk: Thiazide diuretics (Chlorthalidone) have the greatest effect on serum glucose compared to other antihypertensives[span_34](end_span)[span_35](end_span).

text: A diagnostic test has higher PPV than a screening test despite the same sensitivity. Why?
rationale: Dx: Positive Predictive Value (PPV) is directly related to disease prevalence in the tested population[span_36](end_span)[span_37](end_span).

text: 34yo smoker with family history of RA. Which intervention most likely reduces her risk of developing RA?
rationale: Risk: Smoking is a provocative factor for seropositive RA. [span_38](start_span)[span_39](start_span)Risk decreases as the interval of smoking cessation increases[span_38](end_span)[span_39](end_span).

text: 58yo man, 20yr GERD history, smoker, obese. Initial management to assess for cancer risk?
rationale: Dx: Upper endoscopy is indicated to screen for Barrett esophagus in men >50 with >5yr GERD and risk factors[span_40](end_span)[span_41](end_span).

text: 42yo woman with symptoms after MVA 6 months ago. What distinguishes PTSD from Acute Stress Disorder?
rationale: Dx: PTSD requires duration >1 month. [span_42](start_span)[span_43](start_span)Acute stress disorder lasts less than 4 weeks[span_42](end_span)[span_43](end_span).

text: 67yo man with knee 'pop', inferiorly displaced patella (patella baja), unable to extend knee. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Quadriceps tendon rupture. [span_44](start_span)[span_45](start_span)Note: Patellar tendon rupture causes patella alta (high-riding)[span_44](end_span)[span_45](end_span).

text: 22yo female collapses after laughter (no loss of consciousness). 4mo daytime sleepiness, vivid dreams. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Narcolepsy. [span_46](start_span)[span_47](start_span)Characterized by cataplexy (muscle tone loss with emotion) and daytime sleepiness[span_46](end_span)[span_47](end_span).

text: Best next step to diagnose the cause of symptoms in the 22yo female with suspected narcolepsy?
rationale: Dx: Polysomnography (sleep study) can diagnose narcolepsy by showing reduced REM latency[span_48](end_span).

text: Most appropriate treatment for daytime somnolence in the 22yo narcolepsy patient?
rationale: Tx: Modafinil is a first-line treatment for somnolence in narcolepsy[span_49](end_span).

text: 2yo boy, painless brick-red rectal bleeding. Abdomen exam normal. Most likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Meckel diverticulum is the most common malformation presenting as painless brick-red bleeding[span_50](end_span).

text: 25yo woman restarted on valproate after a seizure. How long seizure-free before resuming driving?
rationale: Tx: Patients must be seizure-free (typically 6-12 months) before resuming driving per regulations[span_51](end_span).
